DescribeRequest.description: Request to get the usage contract for a resource.

_meta.description: See [General fields] for notes on `_meta` usage.

progressToken.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications
for this request. The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will
be attached to any subsequent notifications.

cursor.description: An opaque token representing the current pagination position.

intentClass.description: The intent class the Agent plans to use.

resourceId.description: The unique identifier of the resource to describe.

version.description:
The version of the resource to describe.

**Multi-version support**: If omitted, the server returns the latest version
of the resource. If specified, the server returns the usage contract for
that specific version. Version numbers start from 1.

intentClass.description: The intent class this contract applies to.

nextCursor.description: An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.

usageContract.description: The usage contract for this resource and intent.

version.description:
The version of the resource schema that was returned.
This is the specific version that was requested (if specified in the request)
or the latest version (if version was omitted in the request).

ExecutionMetadata.description: Metadata about execution performance and characteristics.

consistency.description: Data consistency level of the result.

durationMs.description: Time taken to execute the query in milliseconds.

sourceSystem.description: The backend system that served the request.

Field.description: Definition of a single field in a resource.

isMasked.description: If true, this field is masked/redacted by policy.

isSearchable.description: If true, this field supports similarity search (hint for SIMILAR operator).

type.description: The data type of this field.

cardinality.description: Number of unique values in this field.

hint.description: A hint for the Agent about how to use this field.

samples.description: Sample values for this field (useful for whitelist validation).

vector.description: For VECTOR fields, specifies vector dimensions and distance function.

dimensions.description: Number of dimensions in the vector.

distanceFunction.description: Distance function to use for similarity search.

whitelistOnly.description: If true, only values from provided samples/examples are valid.

IdentityPredicate.description:
An identity predicate used for unique key lookups.
Only allows the EQ operator to ensure exact matching.

fieldId.description: The field identifier to filter on.

op.description: The comparison operator. Must be EQ for identity lookups.

value.description: The value to compare against. Should be a single value (not an array).

IngestIntent.description: Intent for creating or appending new data.

payload.description:
The data payload to ingest.
An array of records (key-value maps).

InitializeRequest.description: This request is sent from the client to the server when it first connects.

_meta.description: See [General fields] for notes on `_meta` usage.

progressToken.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications
for this request. The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will
be attached to any subsequent notifications.

protocolVersion.description: The latest version of the ADP protocol that the client supports.

InitializeResult.description: After receiving an initialize request from the client, the server sends this response.

_meta.description: See [General fields] for notes on `_meta` usage.

instructions.description: Instructions describing how to use the server and its features.

protocolVersion.description: The version of the ADP protocol that the server wants to use.

Intent.description: The Intent structure used in VALIDATE and EXECUTE operations.

IntentClass.description:
READ operations:
- `LOOKUP` - Retrieve a single entity by unique key
- `QUERY` - Retrieve a set of entities using boolean predicates

WRITE operations:
- `INGEST` - Create or append new data entries
- `REVISE` - Update existing entries (full or partial)

IssueSeverity.description:
Severity levels for validation issues.

- `BLOCKING` - Execution will fail; must be fixed
- `WARNING` - Execution will proceed but with side effects

message.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

JSONRPCErrorResponse.description: A response to a request that indicates an error occurred.

id.description: A uniquely identifying ID for a request in JSON-RPC.

JSONRPCMessage.description: Refers to any valid JSON-RPC object that can be decoded off the wire, or encoded to be sent.

JSONRPCRequest.description: A request that expects a response.

JSONRPCResponse.description: A response to a request, containing either the result or error.

JSONRPCResultResponse.description: A successful (non-error) response to a request.

LogicOperator.description: Logic operators supported for predicate expressions.

LookupIntent.description: Intent for retrieving a single entity by its unique identifier.

key.description:
The identity predicate specifying the unique key to lookup.
This should uniquely identify a single entity (e.g., primary key lookup).
Only the EQ operator is allowed for identity lookups.

fieldId.description: The field that can be mutated.

nextCursor.description: An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.

PingRequest.description: A ping, issued by either the server or the client, to check that the other party is still alive.

Predicate.description: A single predicate in an Intent IR.

fieldId.description: The field identifier to filter on.

value.description:
The value to compare against.
For SIMILAR operator, this should be a SimilarValue object.
For other operators, this can be a primitive value or array of primitives.

PredicateCapability.description: Definition of a predicate capability for a field.

fieldId.description: The field this predicate applies to.

usage.description: Whether this predicate is required or optional.

PredicateGroup.description: A group of predicates with a logic operator.

predicates.description:
The predicates to use.

Note: For the "NOT" operator, callers are expected by convention to supply
exactly one predicate or predicate group, but this is not enforced by the
type system and multiple predicates are still allowed here.

ProgressToken.description: A progress token, used to associate progress notifications with the original request.

fieldId.description: The field that can be projected.

QueryIntent.description: Intent for retrieving a set of entities based on criteria.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

RequestId.description: A uniquely identifying ID for a request in JSON-RPC.

_meta.description: See [General fields] for notes on `_meta` usage.

progressToken.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications
for this request. The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will
be attached to any subsequent notifications.

Resource.description: Base resource information shared between protocol responses and curation manifests.

description.description: A brief description of the resource.

intentClasses.description:
The intent classes this resource supports.

**Wildcard Support**: To accept any intent class, you can:
- Explicitly specify `["*"]` (explicit wildcard)

When `"*"` is included in the array, the resource accepts all intent classes
(QUERY, LOOKUP, INGEST, REVISE). If `"*"` is included with other
intent classes, the wildcard takes precedence.

**Empty Array**: If set to an empty array `[]`, no intent classes are accepted.
The resource will reject all requests regardless of intent class. This effectively
disables the resource.

resourceId.description: The unique domain-qualified identifier for this resource.

semanticDescription.description: A detailed semantic description of the resource.

version.description:
The version number of this resource's schema.

**Multi-version support**: A resource can have multiple versions (1, 2, 3, ...).
Version numbers start from 1. When describing a resource, the client may
request a specific version; if not specified, the server returns the latest
version.

ResourceId.description:
A domain-qualified entity identifier using the format `domain:alias`.
The domain prefix acts as a namespace to prevent collisions.

Result.description: Common result type for all successful responses.

_meta.description: See [General fields] for notes on `_meta` usage.

payload.description: The data payload containing the fields to update.

predicates.description: Predicates to identify the records to update.

ServerCapabilities.description: Capabilities that a server may support.

experimental.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the server supports.

supportedIntentClasses.description: Supported intent classes by this server.

SimilarValue.description: Structured value for SIMILAR operator (vector similarity search).

blob.description: Binary content as Base64 or URI reference.

text.description: Text content to search for similarity.

top.description: Maximum number of results to return.

direction.description: The direction of the sort order.

TraceId.description:
A unique identifier for tracing and debugging purposes.
Used to correlate requests, responses, and error messages.

UsageContract.description: The usage contract returned by DESCRIBE.

capabilities.description: Capabilities available for this resource and intent.

issues.description: List of validation issues, if any.

valid.description: Whether the Intent IR is valid.

field.description: The field associated with this issue.
